briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the bankruptcy of fintech middleware company Synapse, raising serious questions about consumer protections in embedded finance.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is driving increasingly niche and unconventional credit card products as companies compete to differentiate in the payments space.

- **[Apple unveils contactless payments via Tap to Pay on iPhone](https://www.apple.com/newsroom/2022/02/apple-unveils-contactless-payments-via-tap-to-pay-on-iphone/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple announced that iPhones can serve as contactless payment terminals, enabling merchants to accept payments without additional hardware.

- **[Bank of England hints at higher rates as Iran war fuels inflation](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cg7p89mp2rjo?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss)** — *BBC Business* — The Bank of England is signalling potential rate increases as the ongoing Middle East conflict drives inflationary pressures across global markets.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Koho users can now buy, hold, and manage crypto](https://betakit.com/koho-users-can-now-buy-hold-and-manage-crypto/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian fintech challenger bank KOHO has added cryptocurrency buying, holding, and management features directly within its app, expanding its embedded finance offerings.

- **[Bank of Canada holds key interest rate at 2.25%, warning future decisions are clouded by uncertainty](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/bank-of-canada-interest-rate-april-2026-9.7181093?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — The Bank of Canada maintained its benchmark rate at 2.25% while cautioning that ongoing geopolitical and economic uncertainty makes the path forward unclear for Canadian borrowers and lenders.

- **[Canada selected as host country for new global defence bank](https://financialpost.com/fp-finance/canada-host-country-new-global-defence-bank)** — *Financial Post* — Canada has been chosen to headquarter a new multinational defence bank, with Toronto, Ottawa, Vancouver, and Montreal competing to be the host city — a significant win for Canada's financial sector.

- **[AI strategy pillars, new SMB procurement program revealed in Canada's Spring Economic Update](https://betakit.com/canadian-spring-economic-update-2026/)** — *BetaKit* — The Liberal government's spring economic snapshot included new AI strategy initiatives and a small business procurement program with implications for Canada's broader tech and fintech ecosystem.

- **[7 key takeaways from the Liberal government's spring economic snapshot](https://www.cbc.ca/news/politics/takeaways-from-spring-economic-update-carney-9.7180305?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — CBC breaks down the Carney government's spring fiscal update, highlighting key spending priorities and economic signals relevant to Canadian businesses and financial markets.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada's early push to replace screen scraping with secure open banking APIs remains a critical backdrop for understanding where Canadian open banking regulation stands today — still highly relevant as Atlantic fintechs prepare for framework implementation.

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive analysis of how open banking reshapes payments competition, offering useful framing for Atlantic Canadian credit unions and fintechs navigating Canada's evolving open banking landscape.

- **[Lightspeed offloads Upserve at a discount as transformation plan continues](https://betakit.com/lightspeed-offloads-upserve-at-a-discount-as-transformation-plan-continues/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian payments and commerce platform Lightspeed continues restructuring by divesting assets, a reminder of the consolidation pressures facing fintech companies and a signal worth watching for Atlantic startups in the payments space.
